lead.titleZh: 胕绳肌损伤后和膝骨关节炎的最新系统综述值得关注
lead.titleEn: Updates on hamstring injury and knee osteoarthritis: Two new systematic reviews
lead.paragraphZh: 今日有 7 篇文章，涵盖 5 个专业方向。有两篇并列最高 curatedScore 80 分的系统综述，一篇关于胕绳肌损伤后重返运动的加权标准，另一篇是关于短波透热疗法治疗膝骨关节炎。运动康复和手法与理疗领域的临床医师应优先关注这两项系统综述。
lead.paragraphEn: Today's 7 articles span 5 specialties. Two systematic reviews share the highest curatedScore of 80, one on weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury, and another on shortwave diathermy for knee osteoarthritis. Clinicians in sports rehabilitation and manual therapy/physical modalities should prioritize these two systematic reviews.

items.title: Total Knee Arthroplasty (TKA)
items.summary: The APTA provides an evidence-based clinical summary for Total Knee Arthroplasty (TKA), offering guidance for patient care.
items.curatedReason: 这份来自 APTA 的全膝关节置换（TKA）临床总结，为临床医生提供了一个快速查阅的循证资源。你可以用它来核对当前的最佳实践，或作为新入职治疗师的参考。
items.titleZh: 全膝关节置换术 (TKA) 临床总结。
items.summaryZh: APTA 发布了一份关于全膝关节置换术 (TKA) 的循证临床总结，为患者护理提供指导。
items.curatedReasonEn: This APTA clinical summary on Total Knee Arthroplasty (TKA) provides a quick-reference, evidence-based resource for clinicians. You can use it to verify current best practices or as a reference for new therapists.

items.title: Neurogenic bowel dysfunction in multiple sclerosis: clinical determinants and its association with bladder dysfunction in a multicenter study
items.summary: This multicenter study aimed to determine the prevalence and severity of neurogenic bowel dysfunction (NBD) in multiple sclerosis (MS) and identify clinical factors associated with moderate-to-severe bowel dysfunction and its association with bladder dysfunction.
items.curatedReason: 多发性硬化（MS）患者的神经源性肠道功能障碍是一个常见且影响生活质量的问题。这项多中心研究将帮助你识别与中重度肠道功能障碍相关的临床因素，并提示其与膀胱功能障碍的关联，指导你进行更全面的评估和管理。
items.titleZh: 多发性硬化症中的神经源性肠道功能障碍：一项多中心研究的临床决定因素及其与膀胱功能障碍的关联。
items.summaryZh: 这项多中心研究旨在确定多发性硬化症（MS）中神经源性肠道功能障碍（NBD）的患病率和严重程度，并识别与中重度肠道功能障碍相关的临床因素及其与膀胱功能障碍的关联。
items.curatedReasonEn: Neurogenic bowel dysfunction (NBD) is a common and quality-of-life-impacting issue for MS patients. This multicenter study will help you identify clinical factors associated with moderate-to-severe NBD and its link to bladder dysfunction, guiding more comprehensive assessment and management.
items.limitation: 研究设计为观察性研究，不能确立因果关系。
items.limitationEn: The study design is observational, which cannot establish causality.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: Cortical activation and functional connectivity during thumb-to-finger opposition in patients with stroke with varying functional levels: insights from fNIRS.
items.summary: A study with 52 stroke patients found varying cortical activation and functional connectivity during thumb-to-finger opposition, assessed by fNIRS, depending on hand function level and dominance.
items.curatedReason: 这项fNIRS研究揭示了卒中患者在拇指对指任务中，大脑皮层激活模式和功能连接随手功能水平和手优势侧的变化。这些神经生理学见解有助于你理解不同功能恢复阶段患者的皮层重组机制，并可能指导更精细化的神经康复策略。
items.titleZh: fNIRS 研究揭示卒中患者在拇指对指任务中，皮层激活和功能连接随功能水平变化
items.curatedReasonEn: This fNIRS study reveals changes in cortical activation patterns and functional connectivity during a thumb-to-finger opposition task in stroke patients, varying with hand function level and dominance. These neurophysiological insights can help you understand cortical reorganization mechanisms at different stages of functional recovery and potentially guide more refined neurorehabilitation strategies.
items.limitation: 研究样本量相对较小（52名患者），且仅限于右利手患者。
items.limitationEn: The study has a relatively small sample size (52 patients) and is limited to right-handed individuals.

items.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
items.summary: A systematic review establishes weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury, addressing the high recurrence rate and lack of consensus.
items.curatedReason: 这项系统综述和方法学质量分析，旨在为胕绳肌损伤后的重返运动制定加权标准。了解这些标准能帮助你更科学地评估运动员的恢复状况，减少复发风险，并与教练和运动员进行更有效的沟通。
items.titleZh: 胕绳肌损伤后重返运动的加权标准：系统综述与方法学质量分析
items.curatedReasonEn: This systematic review and methodological quality analysis aims to establish weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury. Understanding these criteria will help you more scientifically assess an athlete's recovery status, reduce recurrence risk, and communicate more effectively with coaches and athletes.
items.limitation: 未明确给出综述纳入研究的具体数量和异质性。
items.limitationEn: The review does not explicitly state the number of included studies or their heterogeneity.

items.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
items.summary: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 17 trials (n=1372) found shortwave diathermy significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]) and walking ability (mean -0.97; 95% CI [-1.30, -0.65]) for knee osteoarthritis.
items.curatedReason: 这项纳入17项试验（1372名参与者）的系统综述和荟萃分析证实，短波透热疗法（SWD）显著改善了膝骨关节炎（KOA）患者的疼痛（平均-2.13；95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]）和步行能力（平均-0.97；95% CI [-1.30, -0.65]）。如果你的KOA患者对其他干预反应不佳，或作为辅助治疗，SWD值得考虑。
items.titleZh: 短波透热疗法治疗膝骨关节炎：一项系统综述与荟萃分析
items.curatedReasonEn: This systematic review and meta-analysis of 17 trials (1372 participants) confirms that Shortwave Diathermy (SWD) significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]) and walking ability (mean -0.97; 95% CI [-1.30, -0.65]) in knee osteoarthritis (KOA) patients. If your KOA patients are not responding well to other interventions, or as an adjunctive treatment, SWD is worth considering.
items.limitation: 研究未明确报告疼痛和步行能力改善的临床最小重要差异（MCID），影响临床实践的解读。
items.limitationEn: The study did not explicitly report the Minimum Clinically Important Difference (MCID) for pain and walking ability improvements, which impacts clinical interpretation.

items.title: Elevance pays CMS $342M in midst of Medicare Advantage probe
items.summary: Elevance Health paid CMS $342 million amid a Medicare Advantage probe, signaling increased scrutiny on overpayments and compliance in the MA program.
items.curatedReason: Elevance Health 在 Medicare Advantage 调查期间向 CMS 支付 3.42 亿美元，这表明 Medicare Advantage 的报销审查正在收紧。你需要关注这类合规动态，以避免潜在的报销风险。
items.titleZh: Elevance 在 Medicare Advantage 调查中向 CMS 支付 3.42 亿美元。
items.summaryZh: Elevance Health 在 Medicare Advantage 调查期间向 CMS 支付了 3.42 亿美元，这预示着医疗保险优势计划（MA）在过度支付和合规性方面的审查日益严格。
items.curatedReasonEn: Elevance Health's $342 million payment to CMS amidst a Medicare Advantage probe signals tightening reimbursement scrutiny for MA plans. You need to monitor such compliance trends to mitigate potential reimbursement risks.

items.title: Klomp named to key HHS post
items.summary: Chris Klomp has been named to a key position within the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS).
items.curatedReason: Chris Klomp 被任命为 HHS 关键职务，这一高层人事变动可能预示着未来美国医疗政策和监管方向的微调，值得关注。
items.titleZh: Klomp 获任 HHS 关键职位。
items.summaryZh: Chris Klomp 已被任命为美国卫生与公众服务部（HHS）的一个关键职位。
items.curatedReasonEn: Chris Klomp's appointment to a key HHS post may signal subtle shifts in future U.S. healthcare policy and regulatory directions, warranting attention.
